heading: Repealed. , title XIII, § 1313,July 1, 1944, ch. 37358 Stat. 714
label: 81 to 87 Repealed. , title XIII, § 1313,July 1, 1944, ch. 37358 Stat. 714
notes: Section 81, , , provided penalties for entry of vessels in violation of quarantine laws. See .act Feb. 15, 1893, ch. 114, § 127 Stat. 449section 271 of this title

Section 82, acts , ; , ; , ; , ; , , provided for bills of health. See .Feb. 15, 1893, ch. 114, § 227 Stat. 450Aug. 18, 1894, ch. 30028 Stat. 372Feb. 27, 1921, ch. 8041 Stat. 1149Feb. 7, 1925, ch. 14643 Stat. 809July 10, 1940, ch. 56654 Stat. 747section 269 of this title
Section 82a, , as added , , provided for duplicate bills of health. See .act Feb. 15, 1893, ch. 114, § 13Mar. 3, 1931, ch. 409, § 146 Stat. 1491section 269 of this title

Section 83, , as added , , provided that vessels from foreign ports without bill of health not entering the United States were subject to quarantine regulations.act Feb. 15, 1893, ch. 114, § 11Mar. 3, 1901, ch. 83631 Stat. 1087
Section 84, , , provided for disposition of infected vessels.act Feb. 15, 1893, ch. 114, § 627 Stat. 452
Section 85, , , provided penalties for infractions of quarantine. See .act June 19, 1906, ch. 3433, § 434 Stat. 300section 271 of this title

Section 86, , , prohibited entry of vessels and vehicles contrary to State quarantine laws. See sections 264 to 272 of this title. Compliance with State laws, see .act Apr. 29, 1878, ch. 66, § 120 Stat. 37section 97 of this title
Section 87, , , provided for payment of cost of fumigation and disinfection of foreign vessels.act Apr. 17, 1917, ch. 340 Stat. 6
